name: Baja Fish Tacos
tagline: Beer-battered fish tacos with cabbage slaw and crema
highlight: Crisp fried fish, cool slaw, lime — no shellfish

hero.alt: Baja fish tacos with cabbage and crema
hero.caption: baja tacos — fried fish, slaw

healthFacts: White fish (cod, tilapia) is low-mercury and lean.
Cabbage's crunch holds heat against fryer fish — no soggy taco.

steps.title: Whisk batter and crema
steps.detail: Whisk flour, cornmeal, salt with cold beer until just combined. Separately, whisk sour cream, mayo, lime juice, salt.
steps.tip: Cornmeal in the batter = signature Baja crunch.

steps.title: Fry the fish
steps.detail: Heat 2 inches oil to 360°F. Cut fish into thumb-sized strips, dip in batter, fry 3 min until deep gold. Drain on rack.
steps.tip: Fish strips, not full fillets — taco-friendly portions.

steps.title: Char tortillas
steps.detail: Char corn tortillas directly over a flame or in a dry pan 30 sec per side until soft and spotted.
steps.tip: Wrap charred tortillas in a clean towel — keeps them warm and supple.

steps.title: Build with slaw
steps.detail: Layer fish in tortillas, top with cabbage, drizzle crema, scatter cilantro. Squeeze lime over each.
steps.tip: Two tortillas per taco hold up against fryer-hot fish and saucy crema.
